underlying_goal: Mount Synology NAS network shares (PRO and PRO_UB) to local directories with auto-mount on boot
friction_detail: Initial SMBv3.0 mount failed requiring downgrade to SMBv2.0, and user's 'map the whole drive Z:' (Windows terminology) had to be reinterpreted for Linux.
brief_summary: User needed to mount two Synology NAS shares to ~/nas subdirectories with persistent mounting, and Claude successfully set it up after discovering available shares, resolving SMB version issues, and configuring fstab.
